rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE STEWART LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/05/2024 to be reviewed and calendared with the related petition for instructions filed by Norman Stewart Jr. A preliminary review of this petition indicates that the following issues must be adressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the heairng. 3) The petition must state the name and address of each person entitled to notice. See Probate Code section 17201. The petition must also state the names and addresses of all vested and contingent beneficiaries of the trust. See CRC 7.902. 4) The settlors' heirs who are not beneficiaries of the trust, if any, must be served in the manner required by section 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONIA GUTIERREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The administrator was directed at the hearing on 12/27/23 to file an amendment to cancel the disclaimer. (2) A third "supplement" was e-filed on 1/11/24 instead of the amendment canceling the disclaimer. Juan Gutierrez's cancelation or revocation of his disclaimer signed on 10/23/21 has not been filed. (His disclaimer executed on 10/23/21 meant he had no standing to assign the property on 6/18/22.) (3) Proof of service e-filed on 1/11/24 was of notice of just the petition, not any of the supplements that amended the petition. (4) Furthermore, the proof of service showed (a) mailing on 1/9/24, two days before the third supplement was e-filed on 1/11/24, (b) an incomplete verification, and (c) no copy of the instructions for remote appearance attached to the notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/11/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN EDWARD DURHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) The report was not signed or verified by the administrator. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/11/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. These same or similar issues were noted by another examiner in connection with the report of status filed on 6/30/23. If the issues are not addressed before 3/8/24 in time to deliver the proposed order and courtesy copies to the assigned examiner by 3/11/24, an appearance will be required on 3/11/24. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/20/24. (No earlier availability.) 1) The register of actions shows an ex parte petition filed on 2/2/24. A copy of it and a proposed order were apparently not left with the clerk in Room 103, as required by LRSF 14.11 B., in order for the clerk to log it into the tracking spreadsheet and eventually be assigned to an examiner. 2) No notice of the ex parte petition appears to have been filed either. [LRSF 14.14 C.] 3) A cursory review of the ex parte petition online indicates that it does not fully meet the requirement of CRC 7.52. [CRC 7.52: "Petitioner must file a declaration describing efforts made to locate a person entitled to notice in a proceeding under the Probate Code, but whose address is unknown, before the court will prescribe an alternate form of notice or dispense with notice under (c). The declaration must state the name of the person whose address is unknown, the last known address of the person, the approximate date when the person was last known to reside there, the efforts made to locate the person, and any facts that explain why the person's address cannot be obtained. The declaration must include a description of the attempts to learn of the person's business and residence addresses by: (1) Inquiry of the relatives, friends, acquaintances, and employers of the person entitled to notice and of the person who is the subject of the proceeding; (2) Review of appropriate city telephone directories and directory assistance; and (3) Search of the real and personal property indexes in the recorder's and assessor's offices for the county where the person was last known or believed to reside."] Deadline is 3/11/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN A. DOUGLAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice (or waiver of notice) has been filed. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) The Notice of Withdrawal of Attorney Meredith R. Bushnell was not signed by her. (4) Ms. Bushnell must also receive, or waive, notice. [CRC 7.704.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: (A) Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. (B) A petition for final distribution should list in detail, assets to be distributed, not just âjewelryâ when it was specifically itemized and appraised. (C) The petitioner, not the executor, gave notice to the DHCS and FTB on 12/29/22 before he was appointed on 2/21/23. (D) There was reportedly real property taxes paid... but no real property in the estate. (E) When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. (F) In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENYATTA O. SCOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of progress, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/24/2023 - see also tentative rulings for 11/21/2023, 01/02/2024, and 01/23/2024 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Re the proof of service attached to the DE-121 form: i) It must be dated and signed by a person other than the petitioner. See Probate Code section 1261 & CCP section 1013a. ii) Item 4a must specify the date of mailing. iii) Item 4b must specify the city and state from which required papers were mailed. 3) No proof of publication is on file. 4) Item 2a of the petition must be unchecked, if the decedent died intestate. 5) Item 2b(3) must be checked, if the decedent died intestate. 6) Item 2d: At least one box must be checked. 7) Item 3d: i) Please provide a brief description of personal property. ii) If the estimated value of personal property does not exceed $184,500, please explain why the heirs are not proceeding with the small estate affidavit nonprobate procedure under Probate Code sections 13100 et seq. 8) Item 3e(4) must be unchecked, as it does not apply to the petitioner. If all other heirs waived the petitioner's bond, item 3e(3) must be checked, and DE-142 forms (bond waivers) signed by the heirs (other than the petitioner) must be filed. 9) Item 3g(2)(a) must be checked, if the decedent died intestate. 10) Item 3g(2)(c) must be checked, with the petitioner's relationship to the decedent specified. 11) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 12) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 13) Item 6b must be checked, if the decedent was survived by siblings. 14) Item 9: There were no attachments (4 pages) to the petition. 15) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. 16) A declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate and describe such liabilities and claims. A minimum bond ($20,000) is required for a personal representative who does not reside in California, regardless of bond waivers and absence of any liabilities or claims. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILTON DEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of progress, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/24/2023 - see also tentative rulings for 11/21/2023 & 01/02/2024 hearings) remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 11/01/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 5) No proof of publication is on file. 6) The petition caption: The IAEA box must be checked. 7) Item 2a must be unchecked, if the decedent died intestate. 8) Item 3a: The decedent died more than 4 years ago. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. 9) Item 3e(3): No bond waiver (DE-142 form) signed by the decedent's sister is on file. Item 4 of the DE-142 form must specify the petitioner's name. 10) Item 3g(1)(c) must be unchecked, if the decedent died intestate. 11) Item 3h(3) must be checked. 12) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 13) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 14) Item 8: Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
